draft.title: a CRM for freelancers
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a CRM for freelancers

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer a conversational, approachable voice over corporate formality in their CRM
facade.label: Casual chat tone
facade.acceptImplies: Voice should use contractions, humor, and conversational language; adopt a peer-to-peer rather than vendor-to-customer tone
facade.rejectImplies: Voice should be polished, formal, and professional; maintain clear boundaries between tool and user with structured, businesslike language

evidence.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer a conversational, approachable voice over corporate formality in their CRM
evidence.implication: Voice should use contractions, humor, and conversational language; adopt a peer-to-peer rather than vendor-to-customer tone

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer approachable, non-corporate interfaces that feel less formal than traditional CRM tools
facade.label: Soft rounded cards
facade.acceptImplies: Explore warmer, more human-centered aesthetic; test cream/beige tones and curved UI elements
facade.rejectImplies: Probe toward crisp, minimal, professional designs with sharper corners and higher contrast

facade.hypothesis: A CRM for freelancers should feel approachable and calm rather than corporate—soft neutrals create safety and reduce friction in daily tool use.
facade.label: Warm beige cards
facade.acceptImplies: The interface leans toward human, organic, tactile aesthetics; probably muted color palettes, soft shadows, rounded corners.
facade.rejectImplies: The user prefers cooler, crisper, more minimal or modern visual language—maybe bright whites, sharp lines, high contrast.

facade.hypothesis: For a freelancer CRM, a persistent left sidebar organizing client categories + project types, with the main view showing client info as large cards, maximizes quick scanning and relationship context without forcing modal dialogs.
facade.label: Sidebar with cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values persistent context and quick category jumping; they want spatial memory of where clients live, not modal lookups.
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers flat, linear flows; they may want tabs or a search-first interface instead of hierarchical sidebar browsing.

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prioritize glanceable KPIs (revenue, active clients, upcoming deadlines) over detailed transaction history or narrative reports
facade.axisTargeted: Dense data vs key metrics — does the CRM lead with aggregated numbers or detailed records?
facade.label: Dashboard of key metrics
facade.acceptImplies: Build around a metric-first interface: cards showing money in, clients active, proposals pending. Numbers front and center.
facade.rejectImplies: Lead with narrative, timeline, or detailed views (e.g., activity feed, chronological client interactions, conversation threads). Data as supporting detail, not hero.

facade.hypothesis: Freelance CRM users prefer notifications and form submissions that appear instantly without animation, valuing speed and directness over polish in high-frequency interactions
facade.axisTargeted: Motion and Behavior — Instant vs Animated
facade.label: Instant pop-ins
facade.acceptImplies: UI should prioritize snappy, zero-delay interactions for alerts, confirmations, and modal dialogs; users value getting information fast over smooth transitions
facade.rejectImplies: Users want breathing room and visual flow; transitions and staggered animations help them process changes and feel in control rather than assaulted by sudden elements

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers working across multiple clients may prefer a professional, no-nonsense visual identity that signals trustworthiness and business maturity over approachability
facade.label: Dark, serious interface
facade.acceptImplies: Voice and UI should lean authoritative—darker palettes, minimal personality, corporate-adjacent visual language; treats the tool as mission-critical infrastructure
facade.rejectImplies: Confirms preference for warmer, more approachable design; suggests freelancers want tools that feel like peers, not gatekeepers

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a 'blank canvas' CRM or one with visual warmth and personality
Whether user wants tools clustered in one sidebar/dashboard vs. spread across tabs and modals—reveals navigation preference for a freelancer juggling multiple clients
Whether user responds to professional/formal tone vs. casual/encouraging tone—reveals if they want a business-like assistant or a supportive collaborator
Whether user wants a calm, distraction-free workspace vs. energetic, motivational environment—reveals stress tolerance and work style for solo freelancers
Whether user prefers data-heavy dashboards with metrics/charts vs. simple lists and summaries—reveals how much visibility into business health they need
Whether user wants instant feedback and snappy interactions vs. smooth, deliberate animations—reveals preference for speed-focused vs. thoughtful interface rhythm
